title: Add MeshCore app compatibility codec vectors
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-9c4y.3 chose a handwritten MeshCore byte-command codec, and project rules require packet formats to have canonical vectors. What needs to be done: create test/vectors/meshcore_app_compat.json (or equivalent) with pinned MeshCore source SHAs, raw frame hex, direction, transport type, parsed fields, expected responses/errors, malformed/oversized cases, and drift notes. Acceptance criteria: vectors cover APP_START, DEVICE_QUERY, GET_CONTACTS, GET_CHANNEL, SYNC_NEXT_MESSAGE, battery/storage, time, channel/direct text, serial/TCP framing, BLE raw frames, unknown commands, and at least one SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG ACK drift case; future C tests consume the vectors or generated fixture header.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.13 command matrix: vectors must include all mandatory phase-1 supported reads, representative unsupported commands from each category, response/error envelopes with MeshCore error bytes, push MSG_WAITING, V3 message receive frames, default flood scope null, channel-send ACK drift, and newer firmware-only commands 0x3c, 0x3e, 0x3f, 0x40, 0x41.
Review correction from project-LICHEN-9c4y.13: vectors must include CMD_SEND_SELF_ADVERT (0x07) -> RESP_CODE_ERR/ERR_CODE_UNSUPPORTED_CMD, out-of-range unknown commands below/above the defined range (at least 0x00 and 0xff, preferably 0x42 too), and a documented ERR_CODE_FILE_IO_ERROR (5) fixture only if/when compatibility-store failure behavior exists. Do not include an OK/no-op self-advert vector.
Implementation evidence 2026-07-01: added generated canonical MeshCore app-compat vectors in test/vectors/meshcore_app_compat.json via test/vectors/generate.py; schema.json now defines meshcore_app_compat_vector; README documents BLE/NUS raw inner frames, serial 0x3c/0x3e uint16_le framing, deterministic responses, variable prefix/length responses, and incoming MSG_WAITING/CHANNEL_MSG_RECV_V3/PUSH_SEND_CONFIRMED events. Python tests now validate schema, generator parity, MeshCore wire semantics, serial framing lengths, fixed response error shapes, and response_prefix/response_len metadata. Zephyr meshcore_adapter now generates meshcore_vectors.h from JSON and replays 22 deterministic adapter command vectors from the 28-vector source set. Validation: local 'cd python && PYTHONPATH=src ../python/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_vectors.py' PASS 74/74. EC2 i-05e27e074db786e22 clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y4: direct 'west build -p always -b native_sim lichen/tests/meshcore_adapter -d build-meshcore-adapter-vectors -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen && west build -d build-meshcore-adapter-vectors -t run' PASS 15/15; Twister 'python3 /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN/zephyr/scripts/twister -T lichen/tests/meshcore_adapter -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-meshcore-vectors --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen' PASS 1/1 scenario, 15 cases, 0 warnings. Reviews: Turing no P0/P1/P2; Aristotle found P2 response_len not enforced for variable-response metadata; fixed by schema requiring response_len with response_prefix and Python asserting prefix fits declared length; revalidated Python 74/74 plus EC2 direct/Twister green. Hilbert no P0/P1/P2; noted below-P2 residual that variable time/push/serial vectors are Python/schema-driven and covered separately by existing C tests rather than generated fixed-response C table. git diff --check PASS.
close_reason: Completed: MeshCore app-compat vectors added under test/vectors/meshcore_app_compat.json, schema/generator/Python validation updated, and Zephyr meshcore_adapter consumes the deterministic adapter subset from generated JSON fixtures. Incoming MSG_WAITING, CHANNEL_MSG_RECV_V3, PUSH_SEND_CONFIRMED, and NO_MORE_MESSAGES vectors included.
